build_database package

Subpackages

  • build_database.databases package
    • Module contents

Submodules

build_database.blocks module

BLOCKS required for LLNL thermo database.

build_database.clean_tables module

Uses Solution and Master Solution parsers to compile tables from multiple databases

build_database.clean_tables.clean_tuple(tup: tuple, filter_str: Literal['vm', 'dw']) → Tuple[float, ...] | None[source]

Clean the input tuple by removing elements that contain a specified substring (‘vm’ or ‘dw’) and converting numeric strings to floats. Retains numeric elements as they are.

Parameters:

  • tup (tuple) – The input tuple to be cleaned.

  • filter_str (Literal['vm', 'dw']) – The substring to filter out from the tuple. Only ‘vm’ or ‘dw’ are allowed.

Returns:

The cleaned tuple, or None if the input tuple is empty or contains no valid elements.

Return type:

tuple

build_database.clean_tables.compile_master_solution_table(list_of_databases: list, analysis=False) → DataFrame[source]

Compile the master solution table by merging data from multiple databases.

Parameters: - list_of_databases (list): A list of databases to merge.

Returns: - pd.DataFrame: The compiled master solution table.

Raises: - None

Examples: >>> compile_master_solution_table([‘db1’, ‘db2’]) Returns the compiled master solution table by merging data from ‘db1’ and ‘db2’ databases.

build_database.clean_tables.compile_phase_table(list_of_databases: list) → DataFrame[source]

Compile phase table from a list of databases.

Parameters:

list_of_databases (list) – A list of database file paths.

Returns:

The compiled phase table.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Raises:

IndexError – If the ‘SURFACE_SPECIES’ phase is not found in the result.

build_database.clean_tables.compile_solution_species_table(list_of_databases: list) → DataFrame[source]

Compile the solution species table from a list of databases.

Parameters:

list_of_databases (list) – A list of database file paths.

Returns:

The compiled solution species table.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

build_database.clean_tables.expand_logk(row: Series) → Series[source]

Expand the log_k value in the given row.

Parameters: - row (pandas.Series): A row of data containing ‘log_k’ and ‘delta_h’ columns.

Returns: - pandas.Series: A new series with expanded ‘log_k’ value and corrected ‘delta_h’ value.

Notes: - The ‘log_k’ value is expanded by separating the first element from the rest of the elements. - The ‘delta_h’ value is corrected by removing any entries that do not contain ‘delta_h’ in their lowercase form.

build_database.clean_tables.expand_tc(row: Series) → Series[source]

Expand the ‘t_c’ column in the given row by extracting ‘p_c’ and ‘omega’ values.

Parameters:

row (dict) – A dictionary representing a row of data.

Returns:

The modified row dictionary with ‘p_c’ and ‘omega’ values extracted from ‘t_c’.

Return type:

dict

Notes

This function assumes that the ‘t_c’ column in the given row contains a list of values. The first value in the list is considered as the ‘t_c’ value, followed by optional ‘p_c’ and ‘omega’ values. The ‘p_c’ value is identified by the presence of ‘P_c’ or ‘p_c’ in the list. The ‘omega’ value is identified by the presence of ‘Omega’ or ‘omega’ in the list.

If the ‘t_c’ column is empty or contains only one value, the ‘p_c’ and ‘omega’ values will be set to 0.0. These are the default values listing in PHREEQC documentation.

build_database.clean_tables.log_k_to_float(entry)[source]

Convert log_k value to float

build_database.clean_tables.remove_ones(equation: Series) → Series[source]

Changes 1.0000 to 1 in the equation column

build_database.clean_tables.replace_charges(value: str) → str[source]

Replace charges in a string with their corresponding values.

Parameters:

value (str) – The input string containing charges to be replaced.

Returns:

The modified string with charges replaced.

Return type:

str

Examples

>>> replace_charges("HO----")
"HO-4"
build_database.clean_tables.replace_elements(value)[source]

Replace elements in the format ‘Element(Number)’ with ‘Element(+Number)’.

Parameters: value (str): The input string to be processed.

Returns: str: The processed string with replaced elements.

Example: >>> replace_elements(“H(2)O + Na(1)Cl”) ‘H(+2)O + Na(+1)Cl’

build_database.clean_tables.strfloat_to_stringint(equation: Series) → Series[source]

Removes decimals from equation when representing integers

build_database.clean_tables.tuple_to_float(tup: tuple) → float | None[source]

Converts a tuple to a float by taking the first element

build_database.clean_tables.tuple_to_string(tup: tuple) → str[source]

Converts a tuple to a string by joining all elements with a space

build_database.parser_dat module

Parses phreeqc .dat files and returns pandas dataframes

class build_database.parser_dat.BaseParser(database_file_path: str, section_start: str, section_end: str)[source]

Bases: object

Base class for parsing phreeqc database files

match_patterns(line: str, data_instance) → None[source]

Match patterns in a line and update the data instance accordingly.

Parameters: - line (str): The line to match patterns in. - data_instance (Any): The data instance to update.

parse_block(block: List[str])[source]

Parse a block of lines and return the parsed data.

Parameters: - block (List[str]): The block of lines to parse.

Returns: - Any: The parsed data.

parse_file() → DataFrame[source]

Parse the file and return the parsed data as a pandas DataFrame.

Returns: - pd.DataFrame: The parsed data as a DataFrame.

class build_database.parser_dat.MasterSolutionParser(database_file_path=None)[source]

Bases: object

A parser for processing and combining master solution species data from a database file.

This class handles the extraction of species information from a specified database file and converts it into a pandas DataFrame for further analysis or combination with other data sources.

Parameters:

database_file_path (str, optional) – The path to the database file containing the solution master species data. If provided, the species list will be extracted and processed.

composed_sources

A list of source names that have been processed.

Type:

list of str

data_frame

The DataFrame containing the processed species data. This will be None until the data is processed.

Type:

pandas.DataFrame or None

make_dataframe()[source]

Converts the extracted species list into a pandas DataFrame and assigns it to data_frame.

make_dataframe() → None[source]

Convert the species list into a pandas DataFrame and store it in the data_frame attribute.

The DataFrame will have columns for the element, species, alkalinity, gram formula weight (gfw) formula, and element gfw. The DataFrame will also include a column indicating the source of the data.

Return type:

None

class build_database.parser_dat.PhaseData(phase_name: str | None = None, dissolution_reaction: str | None = None, log_k: List | None = None, delta_h: List | None = None, analytic: List | None = None, v_m: List | None = None, t_c: List | None = None, p_c: List | None = None, omega: List | None = None)[source]

omega: List = None
p_c: List = None
phase_name: str = None
t_c: List = None
v_m: List = None

Bases: BaseParser

Class for parsing phase species in phreeqc database files

parse_block(block: List[str]) → PhaseData[source]

Parse a block of lines and return the parsed data.

Parameters: - block (List[str]): The block of lines to parse.

Returns: - PhaseData: The parsed data.

class build_database.parser_dat.SolutionData(equation: str | None = None, log_k: float | None = None, delta_h: List[float] | None = None, gamma: List[float] | None = None, d_w: List[float] | None = None, v_m: List[float] | None = None, millero: List[float] | None = None, activity_water: List[float] | None = None, add_logk: List[float] | None = None, llnl_gamma: List[float] | None = None, co2_llnl_gamma: List[float] | None = None, erm_ddl: List[float] | None = None, no_check: List[float] | None = None, mole_balance: List[float] | None = None)[source]

gamma: List[float] = None

build_database.parser_dat.file_name(file_path: str) → str[source]

Extracts the file name from a file path

build_database.utils module

build_database.utils.add_hash_to_source(source: str) → str[source]

Add a hash symbol to the beginning of the source if it is not already present.

Parameters: source (str): The source string to add a hash symbol to.

Returns: str: The modified source string with a hash symbol added.

build_database.utils.compile_and_rank_mst(db_list: str, rank: dict) → DataFrame[source]

Compile and rank the master solution table. :param db_list: A list of database names. :type db_list: list

Returns:

The compiled and ranked master solution table.

Return type:

pandas.DataFrame

Notes

This function compiles the master solution table using the given list of database names. It assigns a priority to each row in the table based on the ‘source’ column. The priority is determined by looking up the ‘source’ value in the RANK dictionary. If the ‘source’ value is not found in the RANK dictionary, a default priority of 5 is assigned. The resulting table is then sorted by the ‘priority’ column.

build_database.utils.find_and_collect_matches(series: Series, entry: str, all_match_indexes: list) → None[source]

Find and collect matches in a series based on a given entry.

Parameters:

seriespandas.Series

The series to search for matches.

entrystr

The entry to search for in the series.

all_match_indexeslist

The list to collect all the match indexes.

Returns:

None

Raises:

None

Notes:

  • If the entry is ‘Sb(OH)6-’, a warning message will be logged.

  • The function searches for matches in the series based on the entry.

  • The match indexes are collected in the all_match_indexes list.

build_database.utils.get_missing_species(mst: DataFrame, result_mst: DataFrame, add: str = '#minteq.v4.dat') → DataFrame[source]

Get the missing species from the master database.

Parameters:

  • mst (pd.DataFrame) – The master database.

  • result_mst (pd.DataFrame) – The result master database.

  • add (str, optional) – The source to add. Defaults to ‘#minteq.v4.dat’.

Returns:

The missing species from the master database.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

build_database.utils.phreeqc_database_list(database_directory: str, ignore=None) → list[source]

Returns a list of phreeqc database file paths

build_database.utils.process_missing_species(missing_species: DataFrame) → DataFrame[source]

Process missing species data by removing rows with species in TO_DROP.

Parameters:

missing_species (pandas.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing missing species data.

Returns:

DataFrame with rows removed where species is in TO_DROP.

Return type:

pandas.DataFrame

build_database.utils.remove_hash_from_source(source: str) → str[source]

Remove the hash symbol from the beginning of the source string.

Parameters: source (str): The source string.

Returns: str: The source string without the hash symbol at the beginning.

build_database.utils.reorder_file_list(file_list: str, rank_dict: dict) → list[source]

Reorders a list of file paths based on a ranking dictionary.

Parameters: - file_list (list): A list of file paths. - rank_dict (dict): A dictionary containing the ranking information.

Returns: - sorted_file_paths (list): A list of file paths sorted according to the ranking dictionary.

Example: >>> file_list = [‘/path/to/file1.txt’, ‘/path/to/file2.txt’, ‘/path/to/file3.txt’] >>> rank_dict = {‘#file1.txt’: 3, ‘#file2.txt’: 1, ‘#file3.txt’: 2} >>> reorder_file_list(file_list, rank_dict) [‘#file2.txt’, ‘#file3.txt’, ‘#file1.txt’]

build_database.utils.save_master_database(output_file=None, result_mst: DataFrame | None = None, result_sp: DataFrame | None = None) → str[source]

Save the master database to a file or return the file content as a string.

Parameters:

  • output_file (str, optional) – The path to the output file. If provided,

  • None. (Defaults to)

  • result_mst (pd.DataFrame, optional) – The DataFrame containing the solution master species data.

  • None.

  • result_sp (pd.DataFrame, optional) – The DataFrame containing the solution species data. Defaults to None.

Returns:

The content of the master database file if output_file is not provided.

Return type:

str

Raises:

  • ValueError – If both result_mst and result_sp are None.

  • UserWarning – If either result_mst or result_sp is None.

Notes

  • If output_file is provided, the master database will be saved to the specified file.

  • If output_file is not provided, the content of the master database file will be returned as a string.

  • If either result_mst or result_sp is None, a warning will be raised.

build_database.write_dataframes module

This module contains functions to write data to a file.

build_database.write_dataframes.write_mst(row: Series, file) → None[source]

Writes a row of data to a file.

Parameters:

  • row (pd.Series) – The row of data to be written.

  • file – The file object to write the data to.

Returns:

None

build_database.write_dataframes.write_phase(row, file)[source]

Writes PHASES information to file.

Parameters:

  • row (pandas.Series) – The row containing the phase information.

  • file (file object) – The file to write the information to.

Returns:

None

build_database.write_dataframes.write_sp(row: Series, file) → None[source]

Writes SOLUTION_SPECIES to file.

Parameters:

  • row (pd.Series) – A row of the combined SOLUTION_SPECIES dataframe.

  • file (file) – The file object to write the data to.

Returns:

None

build_database.write_dataframes.write_tuple(att: str, value: tuple, file) → None[source]

Breaks a tuple into individual values and writes them to a file.

Parameters: att (str): The attribute name. value (tuple): The tuple to be written. file: The file object to write to.

Returns: None
